title: The Towers of High Sorcery
subtitle: A Choose Your Own Adventure Prelude

white.name: White
negative: This is not the path of light.

pages.title: THE THREE MOONS OF MAGIC
body: Magic chose you before you chose it. Now the three Moons of Magic demand their due.
Solinari, the white moon, governs magic in service of good — order, protection, the preservation of life. White Robes are the most trusted and the most constrained.
Lunitari, the red moon, governs magic in service of balance — neither good nor evil, but self-determined. Red Robes walk the knife's edge and call it freedom.
Nuitari, the black moon invisible to non-mages, governs magic in service of will — power for power's sake, unashamed of ambition. Black Robes are the most feared and the most misunderstood.
Only the Trials will reveal which calls to you — and whether you survive long enough to answer.

pages.title: THE TOWERS OF HIGH SORCERY
body: Once there were five Towers of High Sorcery, bastions of arcane knowledge scattered across Ansalon. The Cataclysm and the centuries that followed destroyed or corrupted most of them.
Only the Tower of Wayreth remains — hidden in a forest that moves, protected by magic older than the Kingpriests, home to the Conclave that governs all wizards.
Every mage who wishes to practice magic openly must journey to Wayreth and face the Trials of High Sorcery. The Trials are not a test of skill. They are a test of self.
Some who enter do not return. Those who do emerge changed, bearing robes of white, red, or black — their allegiance to a moon declared, their nature revealed.
The Conclave does not create mages. It recognizes what they already are.

pages.title: MILLHAVEN
body: You are from Millhaven, a small farming and fishing village three days north of Vogler. Nothing remarkable has ever happened here. You have lived here your whole life.
Your mother's grandmother, they say, had the gift. It skips generations. Apparently it has decided not to skip yours.
For a week now you have slept badly, waking to a pressure behind your eyes that builds and releases and builds again. You know what it is. You have known for a long time.
This morning, the candle on your windowsill lights itself.
You are about to ruin everything ordinary about this place. You are about to become a mage.

pages.title: THE TRIALS AWAIT
body: As a Wizard, you will master the ancient art of prepared magic — spellbooks, arcane traditions, the patient accumulation of knowledge. Intelligence is your power.
As a Sorcerer, you will channel innate magic that flows through your blood — spontaneous, powerful, shaped by will rather than study. Charisma is your power.
Either way, the road to Wayreth is long and the Dragon Armies are moving. The Conclave will send an escort. The Forest will decide when to let you in.
The Trials will show you who you are.
Magic chose you. Now you must choose what kind of mage you will become.
